canonical_definition: The latent tendency of an LLM to amplify persona-defining semantic features beyond what is topically necessary, contributing to caricature in identity-conditioned narratives.

definitions.text: The latent tendency of an LLM to amplify persona-defining semantic features beyond what is topically necessary, contributing to caricature in identity-conditioned narratives.

relationships.evidence: Recent work formalizes this phenomenon [Caricature] through two measurable components: individuation—the extent to which a response diverges from a neutral baseline—and exaggeration—the degree to which persona-linked semantics dominate beyond topical necessity (Cheng et al., 2023). (Section 2.2)

characterization: The latent tendency of an LLM to amplify persona-defining semantic features beyond what is topically necessary, contributing to caricature in identity-conditioned narratives.
